receipt: License issued June 15, 1921 — Federation Aeronautique Internationale, France. Confirmed: Smithsonian, National Geographic, Britannica.
caption: 1921. The year Bessie Coleman earned a pilot's license no U.S. school would give her. Barred from every American flight school, she learned French, sailed to France, and became the first African American woman to hold a pilot's license. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #bessiecoleman #aviation #blackhistory #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Began drawing c.1939 at about 85 (b. c.1853, est.); 1,000+ works. Confirmed: Smithsonian, Whitney, Britannica.
caption: 1,000+. The number of works Bill Traylor made in about three years, starting at nearly 85. Born into slavery and later a sharecropper with almost nothing, he drew his memories on found cardboard. His work now hangs in the Met and the Smithsonian. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #billtraylor #art #blackhistory #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Traffic signal patent 1923, sold to General Electric for $40,000. Confirmed: Britannica, Smithsonian, National Inventors Hall of Fame.
caption: $40,000. What General Electric paid a man with a sixth-grade education for his traffic signal. Garrett Morgan left school after sixth grade and taught himself. He patented the gas mask and the three-position traffic signal, then sold the signal rights to GE. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #garrettmorgan #invention #blackhistory #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Commandeered the CSS Planter, May 13, 1862; U.S. Congressman 1875-1887. Confirmed: Britannica, Smithsonian, NPS, U.S. House archives.
caption: 1862. The year Robert Smalls sailed a Confederate warship out of slavery. Still enslaved, he commandeered the CSS Planter in the dark, slipped past five harbor forts, and delivered his crew and their families to freedom. He later served five terms in Congress. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #robertsmalls #history #blackhistory #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Little House in the Big Woods, 1932, age 65 (b.1867). Confirmed: PBS American Masters, Britannica.
caption: 65. Laura Ingalls Wilder's age when her first book was published. She wrote small farm columns since her 40s. Reworked after rejection, Little House in the Big Woods came out at 65 and sold more than 60 million copies. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #lauraingallswilder #latebloomer #writing #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Created her hair-care line at 37 (b.1867); first self-made female millionaire (Guinness). Confirmed: Library of Congress, History.com, Britannica.
caption: 37. The age Madam C.J. Walker built her first product, after 20 years as a washerwoman. Orphaned at 7, she washed laundry for about a dollar a day. At 37 she mixed her own hair-care formula and became the first self-made female millionaire in America. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #madamcjwalker #business #blackhistory #realpeoplerealproof

receipt: Thesaurus published 1852 at age 73 (b.1779). Confirmed: Britannica, University of Edinburgh, Wikipedia.
caption: 73. Peter Mark Roget's age when the Thesaurus was first published. He sorted words by meaning for years to steady himself, while working as a doctor. He published Roget's Thesaurus at 73, and it has never been out of print. Real people. Real proof. #thehardwin #roget #latebloomer #words #realpeoplerealproof
